title: Introduction
description: NOC 64400 – Customer services representatives - financial institutions | GTR Immigration Financial customer service representatives (NOC 64400) are the frontline ambassadors of Canada's globally respected banking and credit system. These TEER 4 professionals manage high-volume transactions, resolve account discrepancies, and act as the primary point of contact for personal and commercial clients. This role is a premier entry point for international professionals into the Finance Sector, offering a direct path to transition into high-level roles such as Financial Sales (NOC 63102) or Banking Management.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level4Major Group64 – Sales and service representativesSub-Major Group644 – Customer and information services representativesMinor Group6440 – Customer and information services representativesNOC Code64400

duties: Process customer transactions such as deposits, withdrawals, and bill payments.
Answer inquiries and provide information on various banking products and services.
Identify and promote financial products such as credit cards, GICs, and personal loans.
Resolve customer complaints or account discrepancies and escalate complex issues to management.
Maintain and balance daily cash and transaction records.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Some college or university education in business or a related field is often preferred by major banks.
On-the-job training is provided by the financial institution.
Basic computer proficiency and strong communication skills are mandatory.
job_outlook: The outlook for this occupation is Favourable. While automation and online banking are changing the role, the need for human representatives to handle complex service issues and personalized product advice ensures a steady volume of job openings across Canada.
labour_market_demand: The demand for financial customer service representatives is Very Good, particularly in major financial hubs like Toronto, Montreal, and Calgary. As banks shift toward digital-first models, there is a high requirement for representatives who can navigate complex software while maintaining exceptional interpersonal service. Bilingual candidates (English/French) are in extreme demand for national service centers.
Banking Stability: Work within one of the most secure and stable financial systems in the world.
Professional Development: Canadian banks (Big Five) are known for extensive internal training and tuition reimbursement for further certifications (e.g., IFIC or CSC).
Corporate Culture: High standards of professional ethics, diversity, and inclusion within the workplace.
pathways: BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled: Ideal for those working in the service sector (requires 9 months of work in BC).
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Fast-track for those already working in Alberta in this category with a valid job offer.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A direct route for TEER 4 workers with job offers in the Maritimes.
CEC (Canadian Experience Class): Accessible after completing 12 months of full-time work in Canada.
